faqs.a: Anxious attachment is one of the patterns described in attachment theory. Someone with this style deeply wants connection and also worries it could slip away, so they tend to be highly tuned to a partner's moods and eager for reassurance that the relationship is secure.

No. Anxious attachment isn't a clinical diagnosis and won't appear as a disorder in the diagnostic manuals. It describes a relationship style, a general tendency rather than an illness, and it can shift over time and differ from one relationship to another.

Yes. Attachment styles aren't fixed. People often hold a mix of styles, and an anxious lean can move toward more security through steady, responsive relationships or therapy. No approach promises a particular outcome, and growth tends to happen gradually.
